title: Use of There
tagline: 'कुछ है / कुछ था / कुछ होगा' — There से बताओ!
summary: There is/are/was/were/will be — ये sentences existence (अस्तित्व) बताते हैं। जब आप किसी जगह पर कुछ होने/न होने की बात करते हैं तो There structure use होता है। 'कमरे में एक किताब है' = 'There is a book in the room.' 'क्लास में बहुत छात्र हैं' = 'There are many students in the class.' यह बहुत common और useful grammar pattern है।
hindiSummary: Hindi में हम कहते हैं 'कमरे में एक पंखा है, कई कुर्सियाँ हैं, कल meeting थी, कल शाम एक party होगी।' English में इन सभी को There is/are/was/were/will be से express करते हैं। यह structure English में हर जगह use होता है — descriptions, news, office, travel.
whatYouWillLearn: There is + singular noun (एक चीज़ है)
There are + plural noun (बहुत चीज़ें हैं)
There was + singular noun (एक चीज़ थी)
There were + plural noun (बहुत चीज़ें थीं)
Negative: There isn't / There aren't / There wasn't / There weren't
Questions: Is there? / Are there? / Was there? / Were there? / Will there be?
Short answers: Yes, there is. / No, there aren't.
Quantifiers: There is some / There are many / There is a lot of
Location words with There: in, on, at, near, beside
Common errors: There is many (WRONG) / There are many (CORRECT)
whyImportant: There is/are sentences हर conversation में आते हैं। Job descriptions, news, office communication, travel — हर जगह। 'There will be a meeting tomorrow' 'There is no internet' 'Is there a doctor available?' — यही बोलते हैं हम रोज़।
realLifeUsage: Office: 'There is a meeting at 3 PM.' / 'There are no desks available.'
HR: 'There are 5 open positions in our team.'
Travel: 'Is there a direct flight to Mumbai?' / 'There was a delay.'
Hospital: 'Is there a doctor available?' / 'There are 3 patients waiting.'
Hotel: 'Is there WiFi in the room?' / 'There is no hot water.'
Restaurant: 'Is there a vegetarian option?' / 'There are many choices.'
News: 'There has been a major accident on the highway.'
Phone: 'There is poor connectivity. Can you hear me?'
Email: 'There seems to be an error in the report.'
Meeting: 'There are a few points I would like to discuss.'

dailyGoal.morning: Learn all There forms (is/are/was/were/will be) — 30 min
dailyGoal.evening: Describe your home/room/office using There is/are — 20 min
dailyGoal.night: Write 10 sentences about tomorrow's plans using There will be — 15 min

motivationalQuote: There is no shortcut to success. There are only consistent efforts and daily practice! Keep going!

practiceTarget.speakingTarget: Describe 5 different places using There is/are
practiceTarget.writingTarget: Write a paragraph describing your city using There
